commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=123f900f4fd439d4ebca5bdbb6184775ab235bfa)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `collected.length === 0` guard to archive cron prompt classification in `buildSessionEntry` and a regression test for a mid-transcript `[cron:...]` user message.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +36. Total +37 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current `buildSessionEntry` can classify a usage-counted archive as cron-generated from user text, clear collected content, and skip later messages before memory sync indexes it. I did not run tests or a live memory_search query in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to choose the archive cron-provenance boundary and require real production-path proof before this PR can proceed.

target.title: fix(memory-host-sdk): prevent mid-transcript [cron:...] from wiping archive content
target.body:
## Summary

When a user message starts with `[cron:` mid-conversation (e.g., `[cron:daily-digest] why does the archive vanish?`), the archive content classifier matches it as a cron-generated prompt and wipes ALL previously collected content from the session archive. This makes earlier conversation history invisible to `memory_search`.

Fix: add `collected.length === 0` guard to `isGeneratedCronPromptMessage` check, so only the very first message can trigger cron archive classification. Mid-transcript `[cron:` matches still have their text sanitized via `sanitizeSessionText` but no longer cause cross-message data loss.

Fixes #98241

---

## What Problem This Solves

**Problem**: Users who type `[cron:` as part of a question mid-conversation lose all earlier archive content from `memory_search`.

**Why This Change Was Made**: The regex `DIRECT_CRON_PROMPT_RE` matches any user message starting with `[cron:...]`, including human-typed text. The code's own comment (session-files.ts:858-861) explicitly warns about this scenario but the code ignored it. The fix is a single condition addition: only classify as cron when no content has been collected yet.

**User Impact**: Prevents silent data loss. Users who type `[cron:` prompts mid-session will keep full archive visibility in `memory_search`.

---

## Root Cause

**Trigger condition**: Any user message matching `^\[cron:[^\]]+\]\s*` in a session archive triggers content wipe at `session-files.ts:843-852`.

**Root cause analysis**:
1. `isGeneratedCronPromptMessage` checks `DIRECT_CRON_PROMPT_RE.test(text)` -- returns true for ANY user message starting with `[cron:`
2. The wipe block at line 843 has only `!generatedByCronRun` as guard, meaning it fires on the first match regardless of position
3. `collected`, `lineMap`, and `messageTimestampsMs` are all reset to zero
4. Once `generatedByCronRun = true`, ALL subsequent messages (including real assistant responses) are skipped at line 865

**Affected scope**: All session archive transcripts that pass through `buildSessionEntry` with `allowArchiveContentCronClassification` enabled (i.e., `.reset.*` and `.deleted.*` archive paths).

---

## Linked context

- **Issue**: #98241
- **Related**: PR #70737 (original cross-message drop safety review)
- **In scope**: Archive content cron classification wipe guard
- **Out of scope**: `isCronRunGeneratedRecord` (session-meta based cron detection -- separate code path)

---

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Mid-transcript `[cron:...]` user messages no longer wipe previously collected archive content.

**Real environment tested**: Linux 4.19.112, Node 22, OpenClaw dev checkout

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
pnpm test packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```text
 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  38 passed (38)
   Start at  09:52:13
   Duration  13.49s (transform 7.09s, setup 0ms, import 11.06s, tests 2.07s, environment 0ms)
```

**Observed result after the fix**: All 38 tests pass, including the new test case "does not wipe archive content when [cron:...] appears mid-transcript" which specifically verifies the fix scenario.

**What was not tested**: E2E test with a real OpenClaw gateway and `memory_search` invocation. The unit test covers the exact code path comprehensively.

---

## Tests and validation

### Unit tests

```text
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  38 passed (38)
```

New test: "does not wipe archive content when [cron:...] appears mid-transcript"
  - Sets up a 4-message transcript: user, assistant, user(with [cron:...]), assistant
  - Verifies all 4 messages are preserved in entry.content
  - Verifies entry.generatedByCronRun is not set

### Existing cron archive tests (all pass unchanged)
- keeps cron-run deleted archives opaque when the live session store entry is gone
- keeps cron-run reset archives opaque when session metadata preserves the cron key
- classifies active entries through cron parentage chains

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations
- [ ] I have updated relevant documentation
- [ ] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary

**Session-state risk**: Minimal. The change is strictly additive -- an extra condition (`collected.length === 0`) that only restricts when the cron-classification wipe can fire. Existing cron-run archives where `[cron:` IS the first message are unaffected. The new behavior only prevents false positives on mid-transcript matches.
